element.description: Circled numeral '4' marking a vertical post/member at the eave edge, same convention as R1
Small '0.30' dimension near the post, marking a horizontal offset (left of the sloped roof line, above the post)

warnings: This is eave/fascia detail R2, sibling to R1 on the same sheet A-12.

The circled letter 'E' at the bottom of this detail does NOT match any letter in the building's main gridline set (D, C, B, A) or the dummy-grid set in the master gridline file (mk_test/t3/บ้าน_เล็ก_1ชั้น_01_หน้า00_gridline.json) — this strongly suggests 'E' is a generic local reference/section letter for this detail drawing only, not a proposal for a new building gridline. Not added to the grid master; flagged instead, matching t2's same conclusion independently.

Independent re-read for Gen 4/t3: matches t2's prior extraction on all material callouts, the 1.50 m overhang, and the 'E is not a real grid letter' conclusion. My read of the small dimension near the post came out as a single '0.30' offset value rather than t2's ambiguous 'position/meaning not fully certain' note — kept at moderate rather than low confidence since the number itself (0.30) is legible even though its exact geometric meaning (offset from what reference) is not fully certain.
Sibling detail R1 is in _หน้า14_eave_r1.json.
